# Forms & Intake Module LLM Guide

Purpose: dynamic forms + intake for Cloudflare Workers + D1. Build a form once
(serializable field set), accept validated submissions; powers contact forms,
applications, lead capture, surveys.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: createForm, getForm,
  updateForm, submitForm, listSubmissions.
- `src/validate-submission.ts` is the PURE validator: evaluates conditional
  visibility, coerces values per field type, applies rules; plus validateAttachment.
- `src/ports/` defines FormStore and TurnstileVerifier (spam protection injected).
- `src/adapters/` contains D1 + memory FormStore, a fetch-based Turnstile verifier
  and a memory/always-pass one.
- `migrations/0001_forms_intake.sql` owns forms, form_submissions, and
  submission_keys (idempotency ledger).
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

Model:
- Vertical intake module. Form lifecycle: draft (editable) -> published (field set
  frozen) -> archived (no new submissions).
- A form's fields are serializable data; the same stored definition drives validation.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Submission validation is a single PURE function driven by the stored field set.
  Conditional visibility is evaluated first: a required-but-HIDDEN field is NOT
  required. No second copy of validation in route code.
- Submissions are idempotent via recordSubmissionKey (optional idempotencyKey) so
  a retried POST is stored exactly once.
- Spam protection (Turnstile) runs behind the TurnstileVerifier port; the use case
  never calls fetch. Optional/configurable per form; verifier fails closed.
- Attachments are references only (content-type allowlist + size cap); never bytes.
- Only draft forms can be edited; a published field set is frozen.

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; persistence behind FormStore, spam behind TurnstileVerifier.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject the memory adapters.
- Ask before changing migrations, pii fields, external side effects, or production deploy.
